questions.question: Configure PMM storage and commit behavior.

options.label: Save: Every 5 Messages
options.description: Frequent automatic updates (not recommended - may result in high token usage).

options.label: Commit: Auto-commit
options.description: Commit changes to git after every save (default).

options.label: Commit: Manual
options.description: You decide when to commit (tell the LLM to pmm_save).

questions.question: Which core memory files should be active?

options.label: Custom
options.description: I will specify files later in config.md.

questions.question: How should PMM treat project knowledge?

options.label: Visibility: Private
options.description: No PII restrictions, full fidelity (Recommended if no git tracking or repo is private).

options.label: Priority: PMM-first
options.description: PMM is the source of truth (Default).

options.label: Priority: Coexist
options.description: PMM and OpenCode auto-memory operate independently.

questions.question: Configure the agents handling your memory.

options.label: Model: Haiku
options.description: Fast and cheap for maintenance (Default). In OpenCode, model selection is metadata and the host model executes operations.

options.label: Context: Tiered
options.description: Load core files first, others on demand (Saves tokens, Recommended).

options.label: Context: Full
options.description: Load all active files at session start.
